Name: info_theoreticCompression
Title: Information-Theoretic Limits of Neural Network Compression: A Rate-Distortion Approach
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the compression of neural networks can be understood and optimized through the lens of rate-distortion theory, which provides a fundamental limit on the tradeoff between the number of bits required to represent a network and the resulting distortion in its performance. By developing a rate-distortion framework for neural network compression, we can identify the most compressible parts of the network, allocate bits accordingly, and achieve better compression ratios while maintaining performance.
Related Work: Recent works such as [1-10] have explored the intersection of information theory and neural networks, including the use of rate-distortion theory for model compression [3, 6]. However, these works have focused on specific aspects of compression, such as pruning or quantization, rather than providing a comprehensive rate-distortion framework. Our proposal builds on this existing research and aims to provide a more general and fundamental understanding of the limits of neural network compression.
Abstract: This project proposes to investigate the information-theoretic limits of neural network compression using a rate-distortion approach. We will develop a framework that identifies the most compressible parts of a neural network and allocates bits accordingly, with the goal of achieving better compression ratios while maintaining performance. Our approach will be based on a thorough analysis of the rate-distortion tradeoff in neural networks, and will involve the development of new algorithms and techniques for compression and quantization. We expect our research to lead to a deeper understanding of the fundamental limits of neural network compression and to the development of more efficient and effective compression methods.
Experiments: Develop a rate-distortion framework for neural network compression
Analyze the rate-distortion tradeoff in neural networks and identify the most compressible parts of the network
Develop new algorithms and techniques for compression and quantization based on the rate-distortion framework
Evaluate the performance of the proposed compression methods on a range of benchmark datasets and tasks
Risk Factors and Limitations: The development of a rate-distortion framework for neural network compression may be challenging due to the complexity of neural networks and the lack of a clear understanding of the rate-distortion tradeoff in these systems
The proposed compression methods may not achieve the expected compression ratios or may result in significant degradation in performance
The evaluation of the proposed methods may be limited by the availability of suitable benchmark datasets and tasks
